summary: MTP top-k oracle: the MTP head is excellent at depth-1 (target token in top-8 100% / 96% of cycles), so acceptance is realizable via root-branching trees. But the perfect-accept ceiling stays <1.0 at B<=5 because C_B (ROCm launch floor ~4.25) exceeds B+1. MTP-positive is C_B-bound (dispatch), not acceptance-bound.
method: Instrumented run_native_mtp_proposal to emit per-depth vocab top-k (topk_f32_rows_i32 over lm-head logits); the e2e smoke records the rank of verify.target_top1[0] (target greedy next token) in the depth-1 MTP top-8. HIPENGINE_VERIFY_GPU_ACCEPT=validate to populate target_top1.

B3_D32_exact.note: rank 1 = chain already accepts; 2..k = root-branch tree could rescue; 0 = target token absent from MTP top-k (unrescuable at depth 1).

B3_D64.note: rank 1 = chain already accepts; 2..k = root-branch tree could rescue; 0 = target token absent from MTP top-k (unrescuable at depth 1).

economics_context.avg_accepted_saturates: ~1.4-1.8 (single MTP layer good at depth 1-2, weak deeper)
economics_context.llama_cpp_hipfire: beat AR with C_B~2 (CUDA dispatch/graph ~10x cheaper per launch); same model/acceptance class.

conclusion: The MTP head is NOT the blocker: target token is in top-8 100% (D32) / 96% (D64) of cycles at depth-1. Root-branching trees could realize ~100% depth-1 acceptance.

But C_B > B+1 for B<=5 (launch floor), so perfect-accept ceiling <1.0 at every feasible B. At B=7+ the ceiling exceeds 1.0 but the required acceptance (~84%) exceeds the single-layer head deep-draft horizon.

Therefore MTP-positive requires cutting C_B (~45%, to llama.cpp territory ~2.4), i.e. the megakernel / graph dispatch problem on ROCm. Tree drafts are necessary-but-insufficient: they cannot close a ~2x dispatch gap.
The model is ready; the ROCm launch-submission floor is the wall. Pivot the deployable-win effort to DFlash routing (sidesteps per-cycle C_B).
